_meta.status: DRAFT — REQUIRES INSTRUCTOR CLINICAL REVIEW BEFORE TEACHING USE
_meta.purpose: FR-008 staged-medication-error catalog. GENERIC clinical patterns; the engine intersects these with the active session's formulary, MAR, and documented allergies so suggestions are always injectable in the running scenario. Errors live in session memory only — authored scenario files are never modified.
_meta.sources_note: Sound-alike pairs follow the ISMP confused-drug-names list; interaction and allergy patterns are conservative teaching classics. Review every entry before classroom use.

schema.sound_alike_pairs: {a, b, note} — suggestion fires when EITHER member is on the session's board/MAR (it becomes the intended drug; the other is what gets heard/written)
schema.dose_transforms: {id, display, op:mul|unit_swap, factor?, from?, to?, direction, note}
schema.interaction_pairs: {a, b, risk, note} — fires when one member is on the MAR/board and the other is orderable in the session
schema.allergy_map: {allergen, meds[], note} — fires only when the allergen is DOCUMENTED on the chart and a mapped med is orderable
schema.admin_error_templates: {kind, display, detail_template} — grounded in an actual MAR row at suggest time
schema.impacts: profiles: {id: {display, tiers: {mild|moderate|severe: {symptoms, behavior, vitals{absolute staged values}}}}}; allowed: per error type (wrong_dose by direction, interaction by risk label, admin by kind)

sound_alike_pairs.note: both thrombolytics, both in the formulary — wrong agent/dose protocol

interaction_pairs.note: antiplatelet + anticoagulant (often intended — the CONTEXT note must contradict it)

allergy_map.note: cephalosporin cross-reactivity — LOW risk in modern practice; the teaching point is checking and documenting, not absolute avoidance

admin_error_templates.detail_template: the {drug} in the cart/fridge shows an expiry {days} days ago
MAR shows {drug} due {hours} hours off the ordered schedule
prepared dose of {drug} differs from the MAR ({wrong} vs {right})

mild.symptoms: itching and a spreading raised rash, feeling flushed and uneasy
mild.behavior: scratch at your arms, shift around, mention the itching unprompted once

moderate.symptoms: hives, chest tightness, a little wheezy, lips feel odd
moderate.behavior: audibly short of breath mid-sentence, anxious, ask if something is wrong

severe.symptoms: throat tightening, hard to swallow, dizzy, frightened, breathing is work
severe.behavior: broken short phrases, audible stridor-like effort, visibly panicked

mild.symptoms: oozing from the IV site and new bruising on your forearm
mild.behavior: show the bruise if anyone looks, mention the oozing only if asked

moderate.symptoms: gums bled when you drank water, lightheaded when sitting up
moderate.behavior: move cautiously, mention the dizziness when you sit up

severe.symptoms: blood in your urine, very lightheaded, cold and clammy
severe.behavior: weak voice, frightened, ask for help promptly

mild.symptoms: very sleepy, heavy eyelids, slow to answer
mild.behavior: long pauses before answering, yawn, drift mid-sentence once

moderate.symptoms: can barely stay awake, words slurring
moderate.behavior: one-word answers, slurred, need questions repeated

severe.symptoms: nearly unrousable, breathing feels far away
severe.behavior: respond only to your name and only with fragments, eyes closed

mild.symptoms: lightheaded when you sit up, a bit washed out
mild.behavior: steady yourself on the rail when moving, mention it if asked

moderate.symptoms: dizzy even lying down, vision sparkles when you move
moderate.behavior: decline to sit up, ask for water, sound subdued

severe.symptoms: the room spins, nearly blacked out, nauseated and grey
severe.behavior: eyes shut, brief answers, say you feel like you might pass out

mild.behavior: slow answers, lose your train of thought once

moderate.behavior: slur mildly, ask people to repeat questions

severe.symptoms: can't keep your eyes open, conversation exhausts you
severe.behavior: fragments only, long silences, drift off unless re-engaged

mild.symptoms: the symptoms you came in with are creeping back
mild.behavior: mention that you 'don't feel as good as this morning' once

moderate.symptoms: clearly worse: the original problem is back in force
moderate.behavior: ask whether the medicine is working, visibly uncomfortable

severe.symptoms: as bad as before treatment started, scared it's getting worse
severe.behavior: distressed, ask for the nurse/doctor directly

mild.behavior: fidget, readjust constantly, talk a little fast

moderate.behavior: speak quickly, startle easily, hands visibly unsteady

severe.symptoms: confused and frightened, drenched in sweat, heart pounding
severe.behavior: disoriented answers, mix up details you knew earlier

mild.behavior: press your hand to your chest once, mention it only if asked

moderate.symptoms: runs of racing heartbeat with brief lightheadedness
moderate.behavior: pause mid-sentence when it races, look uneasy

severe.symptoms: pounding irregular heartbeat, chest discomfort, dizzy
severe.behavior: frightened, ask if your heart is okay
